DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Apr-30-2015 Sustained
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances:
Wilda White, in pro se, Tel. 415.748.9868, for plaintiffs;
Bret R. Landess, of Oium Reyen & Pryor, Tel. 415.392.8300, for defendants.

RULING: A R G U E D. The Court adopts its tentative ruling as follows: DEFENDANTS GIRISH SUBRAMANYAN AND SAN FRANCISCO PSYCHIATRISTS, INC'S DEMURRER TO 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT is sustained without leave to amend the demurrer to the intentional infliction of emotional distress and negligent infliction of emotional distress causes of action. Both of those claims are based on defendant providing medical records to plaintiff. California Health & Safety Code sec. 123110(h) provides that a "health care provider shall not be liable to the patient or any other person for any consequences that result from disclosure of patient records as required by this chapter." Once a patient such as plaintiff requests her records (Amended Complaint par. 19), defendant's statutory obligation to turn them over and the concomitant immunity from liability exceeds the 15-day response deadline. In addition, the negligent infliction of emotional distress cause of action fails because it is not a distinct claim. Plaintiff's theory of liability based on the ADHD medication and theory of liability based on the formulation note arise out of the same train of events and are part of the medical malpractice cause of action. See Lawson v. Management Activities (1999) 69 Cal.App.4th 652, 656. As noted, however, there can be no liability based on providing the formulation note under the Health & Safety Code. Order signed in open court. Judge Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk Felicia Green; Court Reporter Denise Doucette, CSR No. 5963; Dept. 302.

Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Answers To Interrogatories; And For Sanctions
Off Calendar Apr-17-2015 Denied
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES: Plaintiff WILDA WHITE, Tel No. (415) 748-9868 present in pro per;
Atty. BRET R. LANDESS of Oium Reyen & Pryor, Tel No. (415) 392-8300 appearing for Defendants Girish Subramanyan, MD and San Francisco Psychiatrists, Inc.

RULING - A R G U E D - The Court adopted its tentative ruling. PLAINTIFF WILDA WHITE'S Motion To Compel Further Answers To Interrogatories and For Sanctions is denied. The underlying request for admission addresses a matter on which expert opinion is required: the standard of care to be observed by Defendant in treatment of Plaintiff. The accompanying Form Interrogatory 17.1 seeks the facts and documents which support the Defendant's denial of the request and therefore delves into matters for which expert testimony is required. Under County of Los Angeles v. Sup. Ct. (1990) 224 Cal.App.3d 1446, 1455-56, questions can be posed to a defendant physician in their capacity as a percipient witness. However, a physician who is the subject of the malpractice action is not required to provide opinion testimony unless the physician is designated as an expert witness. Here, no experts have been declared and Defendant is responding to the interrogatory in a non-expert capacity. Because the underlying request for admission necessarily implicates expert testimony and not facts or details of treatment, the objection to the interrogatory is appropriate. Both parties' requests for sanctions are denied. ORDER SIGNED IN OPEN COURT. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith, Clerk: Sean Kane, Not Reported. (302/EHG)
